title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-24
content:
NAV: $4,305.76  |  Holdings: $3,942.47  |  Cash: $363.29  Week return: +0.41%  vs S&P 500: -0.22%
Market Context
S&P 500 hit fresh all-time record 7,137.90 on April 22 driven by Trump's indefinite Iran ceasefire extension and strong earnings season (Tesla beat, 3 of 4 S&P companies exceeding expectations). S&P pulled back -0.41% on April 23 to 7,108.40; Nasdaq retreated to 24,438.50 (-0.89%) on tech selling and oil price uncertainty. Week-to-date S&P +0.63% from April 17 close. Iran attacked 3 commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22 — the same day the ceasefire was extended — confirming the conflict is unresolved. Hormuz remains closed with $1M+ tolls per vessel and US naval blockade ongoing. Gold under pressure (-8.3% over 30 days), significant ETF outflows in March (largest since 2013), Morgan Stanley downgraded year-end gold target from $5,700 to $5,200/oz. AVGO maintains highest-conviction new-position candidate status (92% analyst Buy/Outperform) but NAV gate ($5,000) remains unmet at $4,305.76.
1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Despite Trump's indefinite ceasefire extension on April 22, Iran attacked 3 commercial ships the same day. Hormuz remains closed, $1M+ vessel tolls, US naval blockade ongoing. GLD 4-week invalidation clock has NOT started. 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — largest monthly gasoline spike since 1967 (+21.2%). Core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12, projected to peak near 4% before declining toward 3% if Hormuz resolves. 3. FED: FOMC April 28-29 expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% ('easy call' per JPMorgan). Policymakers signal 1 cut in 2026 but JPM forecasts NO cuts with possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. U03A stage-down trigger unlikely to fire. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA $1T+ Blackwell+Vera Rubin backlog confirmed (doubled YoY). Vera Rubin in full production on H2 2026 track. AMZN Q1 earnings April 29 — AWS acceleration to 28-30% expected. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Capital rotating from Energy (Q1 leader losing momentum) back into tech and cyclicals. 'Great Rotation' out of tech has reversed. Tech leadership re-asserting at all-time highs despite April 23 pullback. 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues with no new escalation. Trump threatened 50% tariff on countries supplying military weapons to Iran.
Portfolio Health
Grade: B (80/100). NAV $4,305.76. Week return +0.41% vs S&P 500 +0.63% = -0.22pp underperformance — entirely attributable to 8.44% cash drag with S&P at all-time highs. Underlying position performance positive. CSPX fully at target (38.94% of holdings NAV). ASML exit complete. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.74%. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only) — structurally excellent. Key pending catalysts: FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29, MA earnings April 30, May 3 cash deployment hard deadline. GLD WATCH flag elevated — $431.04 with only 2.6% buffer above $420 trim trigger (tightened from 3.6% yesterday). NVDA STRONGEST position at +9.71% unrealized. MA -2.12% unrealized; pre-earnings consolidation, not thesis signal.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single priority action is cash deployment into CSPX before the May 3 hard deadline. Deploy ~$213.29 unconditionally at next available session (April 25 Friday or April 28 Monday open). Reserve $100 for conditional MA add on April 30 earnings beat; reserve $50 minimum cash buffer. If MA add NOT executed: deploy the additional $100 into CSPX by May 3. If FOMC dovish April 28-29: additionally trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). No reductions to existing positions warranted — NVDA and AMZN carry STRONGEST/STRONGER flags, CSPX is at target, GLD and MA are WATCH pending catalysts. Total potential CSPX deployment this week: $213-$313 (unconditional) + $100 (FOMC-conditional) = up to $413.
Cash Strategy
Cash $363.29 (8.44% of NAV) is idle drag, not dry powder, with S&P at all-time highs. Deployment plan: (1) Reserve $100 for conditional MA add on April 30 earnings beat; (2) Reserve $50 minimum cash buffer permanently; (3) Deploy ~$213.29 into CSPX unconditionally before May 3; (4) If MA add condition not met on April 30: deploy full ~$313.29 into CSPX by May 3; (5) If FOMC dovish April 28-29: additionally trim U03A -$100 into CSPX funded separately. Cash must not exceed 5% of NAV after May 3 under any circumstances.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 (when NAV > $5,000)): Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate — AI custom silicon leadership via Google/Meta hyperscaler partnerships through 2029-2031, $73B AI backlog targeting $100B+ AI revenue by FY2027, VMware networking dominance. AI chip revenue projected to grow 100%+ in 2026. 92% analyst Buy/Outperform consensus, median price target $458 (up to $525 Street high). Negligible dividend = 0% effective WHT. Complementary to NVDA (custom XPUs vs standard GPUs). Gate: NAV must exceed $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,305.76 is $694 below threshold — do NOT buy yet.
Action Items
1. URGENT — This week: Deploy ~$213.29 into CSPX at market (April 25 Friday or April 28 Monday open at latest). May 3 hard deadline is 9 days away. Execute unconditionally.
2. CRITICAL — April 28-29 FOMC: If dovish signal (explicit rate cut window), trim U03A -$100 and deploy into CSPX immediately. If hold-unchanged or hawkish: no U03A action.
3. CRITICAL — April 29 (5:30 PM ET) AMZN Q1 earnings: ADD $50-75 if AWS growth >25% AND Q1 EBIT >$20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. No exit unless EBIT below $16.5B floor with Q2 guide-down.
4. CRITICAL — April 30 MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate REDUCE only on material miss + guide-down.
5. HARD DEADLINE — May 3: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. If MA add executed: deploy ~$163; if not: deploy ~$263. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions.
6. DAILY MONITOR — GLD $420 alert: GLD at $431.04, only 2.6% above trim trigger. If daily close below $420: trim 25% of GLD (~$108) and redeploy into CSPX immediately. Trigger could fire within 1-3 sessions.
7. WATCH — NVDA earnings date: Verify Q1 FY2027 earnings date — May 20 vs May 27 discrepancy noted between sources. Prior thesis corrected to May 27. Confirm before that date.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-23
content:
NAV: $4,288.17  |  Holdings: $3,953.77  |  Cash: $334.40  Week return: -0.08%  vs S&P 500: -1.12%
Market Context
S&P 500 hit a fresh all-time record close of 7,137.90 on April 22 (+1.04% from April 21 close of 7,064.01), driven by Trump indefinitely extending the Iran ceasefire and strong Q1 earnings beats (3 of 4 S&P 500 companies exceeding expectations). Nasdaq gained +1.64% to 24,657.57 — tech reclaiming leadership. However, Iran simultaneously attacked 3 commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22 hours after the ceasefire extension, exposing the fragile nature of the geopolitical situation. The 'Great Rotation' out of tech appears to be reversing — CNBC headlined 'The much-hyped great rotation out of tech for 2026 may be over already' on April 22. MILESTONE this week: ASML exit successfully executed and ~$150 of proceeds redeployed into CSPX, resolving the multi-report structural underweight.
1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Ceasefire indefinitely extended by Trump on April 22, but Iran attacked 3 commercial ships the same day. Hormuz remains effectively closed with $1M+ tolls per vessel; US naval blockade continues. GLD 4-week risk-on clock has NOT started — Iran's continued ship attacks confirm the conflict is not resolved. Goldman Sachs projects Brent at ~$80/barrel year-end (~$20 above pre-war baseline). 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — peak driven by Iran war energy costs, gasoline +18.9% YoY. Core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12, expected to peak near 4% before declining toward 3% if Hormuz resolves. 3. FED: Rates held at 3.50-3.75%; April 28-29 FOMC described as 'an easy call' to hold. JPMorgan forecasts no cuts in 2026. U03A stage-down trigger contingent on dovish FOMC signal — unlikely to fire given JPM hawkish outlook. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA confirmed $1T+ Blackwell + Vera Rubin backlog through 2027 (doubled in one year). AMZN Q1 earnings on April 29 — AWS growth expected to accelerate to 28-30%. NVDA up 18% in 10 days. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Tech reclaiming leadership from Energy. 'Great Rotation' into value/energy appears to be reversing. IT, Financials, Materials showing pro-cyclical earnings support. 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues with no new escalation this week.
Portfolio Health
Grade: B (80/100). NAV $4,288.17. Week return -0.08% vs S&P 500 +1.04% = -1.12pp underperformance — entirely attributable to transaction costs from ASML exit + CSPX add commissions (~$8-12 friction); underlying position performance was positive (+~$30 from price appreciation). MILESTONE: ASML exit executed and ~$150 of proceeds redeployed into CSPX. CSPX now at 38.70% of holdings NAV (35.68% of total NAV) — at/above the 36% holdings target for the first time. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.32%. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only) — excellent overall structure. Key upcoming catalysts: FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29, MA earnings April 30, May 3 cash deployment hard deadline. GLD on WATCH (3.6% buffer above $420 trim trigger, improved from 2.3% at April 21 close). NVDA STRONGEST position — up 18% in 10 days.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
ASML exit successfully executed and approximately $150 of proceeds redeployed into CSPX (1.8025 -> 1.9989 shares). CSPX is now at 38.70% of holdings NAV — the multi-report structural underweight has been fully closed. Remaining cash $334.40 (7.80% of NAV) to be deployed as follows: $100 conditional on MA April 30 earnings beat criteria, remaining ~$234 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline (unconditional). FOMC April 28-29: if dovish, additionally trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). NVDA and AMZN both carry STRONGER/STRONGEST thesis flags — no reductions warranted. Portfolio structure is clean with the ASML drag eliminated.
Cash Strategy
Cash $334.40 (7.80% of NAV) is transitional post-ASML-exit dry powder. Deployment plan: (1) Reserve $100 for conditional MA add on April 30 earnings beat; (2) Deploy remaining ~$234 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline unconditionally (retain $50 minimum buffer); (3) If FOMC dovish on April 28-29, additionally trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). Cash must not exceed 5% of NAV after May 3. If MA add condition not met: deploy full ~$284 into CSPX by May 3.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 (when NAV > $5,000)): Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate — AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator partnerships through 2029-2031), VMware networking dominance, AI chip revenue expected to double in Q1 2026. Negligible dividend = 0% effective WHT. The 'Great Rotation' reversal back to tech makes timing increasingly attractive. Gate: NAV must exceed $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,288 is $712 below the threshold.
Action Items
1. IMMEDIATE WATCH — April 28-29 FOMC: If dovish signal (rate cut window opened or cutting bias expressed), trim U03A -$100 and deploy into CSPX immediately. If hawkish or hold-unchanged: no U03A action.
2. CRITICAL — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings (5:30 PM ET): ADD $50-75 post-earnings if AWS growth >25% AND EBIT >$20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. No exit unless severe guide-down.
3. CRITICAL — April 30 MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate partial reduce only on material miss with guide-down.
4. HARD DEADLINE — May 3: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. If MA add executed: deploy ~$184; if not: deploy ~$284. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions under any circumstances.
5. MONITOR — GLD $420 daily close alert: GLD at $435.26, only 3.6% above trim trigger. If daily close below $420: trim 25% of GLD (~$109) and redeploy into CSPX immediately.
6. WATCH — Iran developments: Iran attacked 3 ships on April 22 despite ceasefire extension. Any formal peace deal + durable Hormuz reopening starts the 4-week GLD invalidation clock. Clock has NOT started.
7. MONITOR — NVDA May 27 earnings: Q1 FY2027 consensus $1.75 EPS / $78.8B revenue. Watch HBM supply constraint update for Vera Rubin production ramp. NOTE: Earnings date is May 27 (corrected from prior May 20 reference in thesis).

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-21
content:
NAV: $4,291.59  |  Holdings: $4,049.00  |  Cash: $242.59  Week return: -0.74%  vs S&P 500: -0.50%
Market Context
S&P 500 at ~7,124 on April 21 (up from 7,041 April 17 close); April 20 saw a -0.24% decline on renewed Iran tensions before recovering Tuesday. Nasdaq snapped a 13-day winning streak (longest since 1992). Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19, reversing the brief April 17 opening. Fragile ceasefire expires April 23; Tehran vowing retaliation after US vessel seizure. IEA describes the disruption as the largest oil supply shock in global market history. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM (highest since early 2024); April CPI due May 12 and expected to rise further. S&P 500 is approximately 12.5% above March 30 lows. Eight of eleven S&P sectors reporting year-over-year earnings growth, led by Information Technology, Materials, and Financials. CRITICAL MILESTONE: CSPX $250 ADD finally executed on April 20 (confirmed by position growth 1.4745 -> 1.8025 shares), resolving the 9-report operational failure.
1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Re-closure April 18-19 is the dominant macro event. Ceasefire expires April 23. Tehran vowing retaliation after vessel seizure. Stagflation and recession risk elevated — IEA 'largest supply disruption in history.' US-China tensions rising as blockade cuts Chinese oil imports. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY; April CPI likely higher given Hormuz re-closure. Fed constrained — JPM sees no cuts in 2026, possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. Rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. 3. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues. US-China relations strained by Hormuz blockade impact on Chinese oil. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: Hyperscalers collectively committing $200B+ to AI infrastructure in 2026. $1T in confirmed chip demand through 2027. Blackwell sold out through mid-2026 at $40K/GPU. Vera Rubin (H2 2026) is next inflection catalyst. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Rotation from pure growth to quality value. Tech recovering from Q1 lows. Energy was YTD leader but oil profits evaporating as Hormuz dynamics shift back to closure.
Portfolio Health
Grade: B- (77/100). NAV $4,291.59. Week return -0.74% vs S&P 500 (April 17-20) -0.24% = -0.50pp underperformance, fully attributable to commission drag from CSPX ADD execution ($2-3 friction) and minor CSPX price pullback from $766.84 entry to $762.96 close. MILESTONE: CSPX $250 ADD executed April 20 — 9-report operational failure resolved. CSPX weight improved from 26.2% to 33.96% of NAV. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.40%. NEW CONCERN: ASML crosses into DETERIORATING territory — new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold), stock fell 6% on Q1 beat. EXIT recommended. All other positions have intact or strengthening theses. Upcoming catalysts: AMZN Q1 earnings April 29, MA Q1 earnings April 30, FOMC April 28-29. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only).
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single clearest signal is ASML exit: thesis DETERIORATING with new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% heading below 15% invalidation threshold, stock fell 6% on Q1 beat. Exit proceeds ($254 net) + existing cash ($242.59) = ~$497. Deploy $100-110 into CSPX to close 36% target gap; reserve $100 for MA April 30 earnings add; retain $50 buffer; deploy remaining ~$237 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline. Conditional: FOMC April 28-29 dovish signal triggers U03A -$100 stage-down into CSPX. CSPX is the highest-conviction, most WHT-efficient use of capital at this portfolio size.
Cash Strategy
Current cash $242.59 (5.65% of NAV) — appropriate dry powder level. Post-ASML exit: ~$497 total. Deploy $100-110 into CSPX immediately (close 36% gap). Reserve $100 for MA April 30 add. FOMC April 28-29: if dovish, trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). May 3 hard deadline: deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 8% of NAV after May 3.
New Opportunities
TSM ($0 — gated until NAV >$5,000 AND CSPX at 36% target AND ASML fully exited): TSMC is the foundational pick-and-shovel AI semiconductor play — fabricates all NVIDIA Blackwell and Vera Rubin chips, plus AAPL and AMD. 32% YoY revenue growth forecast for current quarter, 40%+ 2026 earnings growth, forward P/E 25 comparable to Nasdaq-100 but with materially higher growth rate. Natural complement to NVDA thesis. Dividend yield ~1.5% subject to Taiwan WHT (~21% treaty rate — modest drag). Research candidate only — not actionable until NAV >$5,000 AND CSPX at target AND ASML fully exited.
Action Items
1. CRITICAL — Exit ASML full position (0.1739 shares) this week at market (~$256.76 gross). Thesis DETERIORATING: new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% approaching 15% invalidation threshold, stock -6% on Q1 beat. Redeploy $100-110 proceeds into CSPX immediately post-exit.
2. URGENT — April 28-29 FOMC: If dovish signal or rate cut guidance, trim U03A -$100 and deploy into CSPX. If hawkish or hold-unchanged, no U03A action.
3. IMPORTANT — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings (5:30 PM ET): Monitor AWS revenue growth (must sustain >20%), operating income vs $16.5-21.5B guidance range, and Q2 guidance. Beat with raised guidance = review sizing upward. Miss on operating income = HOLD.
4. IMPORTANT — April 30 MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
5. MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions — final deadline.
6. WATCH — GLD: Monitor ceasefire expiration April 23. Formal US-Iran peace deal + Hormuz formally open for 4+ consecutive weeks = begin GLD invalidation timer for 25% trim into CSPX. Current: HOLD unconditionally.
7. WATCH — NVDA: May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings are the next catalyst. Monitor Vera Rubin production ramp confirmation and execution vs $78B Q1 revenue guidance.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-20
content:
NAV: $4,315.88  |  Holdings: $3,813.44  |  Cash: $502.44  Week return: +2.13%  vs S&P 500: -2.41%
Market Context
S&P 500 gained +4.54% and Nasdaq +6.84% for the week of April 14-17, 2026, closing at all-time highs (S&P 7,041+). Nasdaq posted its 12th consecutive winning session — longest streak since 2009. CRITICAL NEW DEVELOPMENT: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz as of approximately April 18-19, citing US breaches of trust — reversing the April 17 reopening that drove last week's equity rally. This reintroduces geopolitical risk premiums and materially strengthens the GLD thesis. AVGO surged +28% on AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031). Dow gained +1.79% on Friday April 17.
Fed held at 3.50-3.75% for second consecutive meeting; FOMC April 28-29 widely expected to hold — JPMorgan now sees NO cuts in 2026 and a potential 25bps hike in Q3 2027 if inflation persists. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM; core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12. Tariff passthrough expected to peak in Q2 2026 — rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. Iran Hormuz re-closure is the dominant new macro development: oil supply risk restored, gold safe-haven bid reasserting, inflation expectations re-elevated. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21-34% YTD, Materials, Industrials leading over Technology — April saw counter-trend tech rally which may reverse with Hormuz re-closure.
Portfolio Health
Grade: C+ (74/100). NAV $4,315.88. Week return +2.13% vs S&P 500 +4.54% = underperformance of -2.41pp, entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag and CSPX structural underweight (26.2% actual vs ~36% target). CSPX ADD recommended for NINTH consecutive report without execution — singular operational failure of this portfolio. YTD since April 4 seed ($4,191): +2.98% vs CSPX YTD +3.16% — now marginally behind benchmark. All 7 positions positive unrealized. Iran Hormuz re-closure STRENGTHENS GLD thesis and partially restores U03A capital preservation value. ASML China mix fell to 19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold — WATCH flag). MA earnings date corrected to April 30 (not April 23). WHT YTD: only $3.33 from MA dividend.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single priority action: ADD $250 to CSPX at April 20 Monday open — unconditional, 9th consecutive recommendation. Post-trade: CSPX rises from 26.2% to ~31.5% of NAV; cash drops from 11.6% to ~5.8%. Subsequent steps: (1) conditional +$100 MA on April 30 earnings beat; (2) U03A stage-down -$100 post-FOMC April 28-29 if dovish signal received; (3) unconditional CSPX top-up by May 3 hard deadline with remaining cash. ASML monitored but not trimmed at current size due to commission inefficiency. Decisions driven purely by current thesis strength and market conditions — not anchored to prior report weights.
Cash Strategy
$502.44 available (11.6% of NAV) — structural drag, not dry powder. STEP 1 (April 20, unconditional): Deploy $250 into CSPX; post-trade cash ~$252. STEP 2 (April 30, conditional): $100 into MA on earnings beat; post-trade cash ~$152. STEP 3 (May 3 hard deadline, unconditional): Deploy ~$102-$152 into CSPX; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3. Iran Hormuz re-closure does not override the deployment schedule.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 when NAV > $5,000): Broadcom surged +28% in April 2026 on landmark AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031) — highest-conviction new position candidate. Custom silicon leadership, VMware networking dominance, $438-480 analyst price targets. Zero effective dividend = 0% WHT. Complements NVDA (custom silicon vs standard GPUs). Gate: NAV > $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,315 is below threshold. Post-surge entry at ~$393 is less attractive than original identification; wait for NAV growth or 10-15% AVGO pullback to $340-355 range.
Action Items
CRITICAL — Monday April 20 open (TODAY): Buy CSPX $250 (~0.326 shares at ~$766.84). NINTH consecutive report. Non-negotiable — no further deferral. Hormuz re-closure may cause brief dip on open; use it as a slightly better entry.
URGENT — April 28-29 FOMC: Watch rate decision and forward guidance. Dovish signal = initiate U03A first stage-down (~$100 redeployed into CSPX, reducing U03A from 13.9% toward 12% of NAV).
IMPORTANT — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings: Monitor AWS growth rate (>20% target), AI revenue disclosure, operating margin. Beat expected. No pre-earnings action required.
IMPORTANT — April 30 MA Q1 earnings (CORRECTED DATE — not April 23): ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$102-$152 post-CSPX and MA trades) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions permitted.
ONGOING — ASML WHT verification: Confirm 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA rate on next IBKR tax document. If 30% confirmed, initiate switch to ASML.AS (Euronext Amsterdam).
WATCH — ASML China mix: Q2 2026 results are the critical trigger. If China mix <15% of net sales OR DUV restriction bill advances materially in Congress, exit full position and redeploy into CSPX/NVDA.
WATCH — GLD: Iran Hormuz re-closure (April 18-19) REINFORCES HOLD. 4-week risk-on clock has NOT started and is now fully reset. GLD $420 price alert remains active for 25% trim trigger. All four invalidation conditions remain untriggered.
NEW DEVELOPMENT — Iran Hormuz re-closure: Monitor closely. If formal peace deal and durable reopening occur, reassess GLD invalidation timeline and accelerate U03A stage-down. Conversely, if conflict escalates, GLD ADD from U03A redeployment becomes higher priority than CSPX.
